title: Rathouse
summary: Art and digital. Exclusivity and accessibility.
desc: An end-to-end product plan for the contemporary art market through two coordinated interventions: an SNS–database hybrid platform and a crowdfunded revenue-share model. Began as a research-driven inquiry into how digital integration is reshaping the contemporary art market. Advanced from structural diagnosis to product logic, translating market-level tensions into a focused design intervention tested through controlled interaction.

desc_items.title: Status
content: Test B designed, not yet run

title: Bol
desc: A recipe application built end-to-end as an exploration of treating baking as structured, manipulable state. Bol reframes recipe design as a unified problem of data structure, interaction, and iteration. Built in React Native with a custom computation layer.

desc_items.title: Role
content: Solo project, designed and implemented end-to-end

title: Let Them Wave Flag
desc: This project reframes thematic investment to match how people actually arrive at it. The intervention works in three layers. New objects: baskets and themes treated as different kinds of holding rather than collapsed into instruments. New surfaces: with standings and themes as separate views over the same portfolio. New flow: compressing the onboarding chain so the user authors two decisions instead of six. The direction started from a preliminary behavioral study and was tested through comparative prototype flows.

title: Twenty CRM
summary: An Inbox that walks the graph.
desc: Two-week sprint with Twenty, an open-source CRM. I designed a notification inbox as a view of the existing Task object. Four primitives: a notification data model, a Focus filter using graph distance, an action panel, and a Resolve-with pattern that closes related notifications in one move. Five-user moderated test on an interactive prototype against a scripted high-stakes scenario. Shipped a live, fully functional hi-fi prototype.
